ABT: Abbott Laboratories is a global healthcare company that discovers, develops, manufactures, and sells a broad range of health care products. The company operates through four segments: Established Pharmaceutical Products, Diagnostic Products, Nutritional Products, and Medical Devices. Abbott is known for its FreeStyle Libre continuous glucose monitoring system, a market-leading diabetes care product, as well as its diagnostics business which expanded significantly during COVID-19. The company has a long history of consistent dividend growth, making it a Dividend Aristocrat.
A: Agilent Technologies is a global leader in life sciences, diagnostics, and applied chemical markets. The company provides instruments, software, services, and consumables for laboratories worldwide. Agilent's products are used in pharmaceutical and biotech research, clinical diagnostics, food safety testing, and environmental analysis. Spun off from Hewlett-Packard in 1999, the company has grown into one of the most important analytical instrument makers in the world.
ADI: Analog Devices is a global semiconductor company specializing in data conversion, signal conditioning, and power management technology. The company designs, manufactures, and markets analog, mixed-signal, and digital signal processing integrated circuits used in virtually all types of electronic equipment. ADI serves customers across industrial, automotive, communications, and consumer end markets. Its 2021 acquisition of Maxim Integrated significantly expanded its product portfolio and market reach.
ATO: Atmos Energy Corporation is one of the largest natural gas distribution companies in the United States, delivering natural gas to over three million customers in eight states. The company operates through two segments: Distribution and Pipeline and Storage. Atmos Energy has a strong track record of capital investment in pipeline safety and reliability, and is recognized as one of the most consistently growing utilities in the country with a long history of dividend increases.
ADP: Automatic Data Processing is one of the largest providers of human capital management solutions worldwide. The company offers cloud-based payroll processing, human resources management, talent management, tax and benefits administration, and compliance services. ADP serves approximately 1 million clients globally, ranging from small businesses to large enterprises, and processes payroll for roughly one in six U.S. workers. The company is a Dividend Aristocrat with over 40 consecutive years of dividend increases.
BKH: Black Hills Corporation is a diversified energy company serving 1.3 million natural gas and electric utility customers across eight states in the western United States. The company operates through its Electric Utilities and Gas Utilities segments, providing regulated electric and natural gas service. Black Hills has a long history of dividend growth, having increased its dividend for over 50 consecutive years, making it one of the longest streaks among U.S. utilities.
CAT: Caterpillar Inc. is the world's leading manufacturer of construction and mining equipment, diesel and natural gas engines, industrial gas turbines, and diesel-electric locomotives. The company operates through three primary segments: Construction Industries, Resource Industries, and Energy & Transportation. Caterpillar products are sold in nearly every country, and the company is widely considered a bellwether for global economic activity. CAT is a Dividend Aristocrat with over 25 consecutive years of dividend increases.
CHD: Church & Dwight Co. is a leading consumer packaged goods company that manufactures and markets a broad range of household, personal care, and specialty products. The company's portfolio includes well-known brands such as Arm & Hammer, OxiClean, Trojan, First Response, Batiste, and Waterpik. Church & Dwight has consistently delivered above-average organic growth in the consumer staples sector and maintains a strong record of dividend increases.
CL: Colgate-Palmolive Company is a global consumer products company focused on oral care, personal care, home care, and pet nutrition. The company's products are sold in over 200 countries under brands including Colgate, Palmolive, Ajax, Hill's Science Diet, and Tom's of Maine. Colgate holds the #1 global market share in toothpaste and is one of the most recognized consumer brands worldwide. The company has paid uninterrupted dividends since 1895.
FAST: Fastenal Company is a leading distributor of industrial and construction supplies in North America. The company operates through a network of approximately 1,700 branch locations and over 1,800 Onsite locations embedded within customer facilities. Fastenal distributes fasteners, safety products, cutting tools, hydraulics, and other industrial supplies. The company's unique vending machine program and Onsite model have driven consistent growth and market share gains in the industrial distribution space.
GD: General Dynamics Corporation is a global aerospace and defense company that offers a broad portfolio of products and services in business aviation, ship construction and repair, land combat vehicles, weapons systems, and technology solutions. The company operates through four segments: Aerospace (Gulfstream jets), Marine Systems (submarines, surface ships), Combat Systems (armored vehicles), and Technologies. General Dynamics is one of the top five U.S. defense contractors by revenue.
GPC: Genuine Parts Company is a leading distributor of automotive and industrial replacement parts. The company operates through its Automotive Parts Group, which includes the NAPA Auto Parts brand in North America, and its Industrial Parts Group, which distributes industrial replacement parts. GPC has operations across North America, Europe, and Australasia. The company is a Dividend King, having increased its dividend for over 65 consecutive years.
LRCX: Lam Research Corporation is one of the world's leading suppliers of semiconductor manufacturing equipment. The company designs, manufactures, and services wafer fabrication equipment used in the production of integrated circuits. Lam's products are essential for etching, deposition, and cleaning processes in chip manufacturing. The company holds dominant market share in plasma etch and has been a key beneficiary of increasing chip complexity and the global semiconductor buildout.
LMT: Lockheed Martin Corporation is the world's largest defense contractor, specializing in aerospace, defense, arms, security, and advanced technologies. The company operates through four business segments: Aeronautics (including the F-35 fighter jet), Missiles and Fire Control, Rotary and Mission Systems, and Space. Lockheed Martin is a primary contractor for the U.S. Department of Defense and numerous allied nations, and generates the majority of its revenue from government contracts.
MATX: Matson, Inc. is a leading provider of ocean transportation and logistics services in the Pacific. The company operates a fleet of vessels providing containership service to Hawaii, Alaska, Guam, Micronesia, and select South Pacific island economies, as well as a premium expedited service from China to Southern California. Matson also operates a logistics business providing multimodal transportation, warehousing, and distribution services across North America.
NEE: NextEra Energy is the world's largest generator of renewable energy from wind and solar, and one of the largest electric utilities in the United States. The company operates through Florida Power & Light (FPL), the largest rate-regulated electric utility in the country, and NextEra Energy Resources, the world's largest generator of renewable energy from wind and solar. NEE has delivered consistent earnings growth and is widely regarded as the premier utility growth stock.
ORI: Old Republic International Corporation is a diversified insurance holding company that focuses on commercial lines of insurance. The company operates through three segments: General Insurance, Title Insurance, and the Republic Financial Indemnity Group run-off business. Old Republic has a long track record of profitability and has paid uninterrupted dividends for over 80 years, making it one of the longest dividend streaks of any U.S. company.
PCAR: PACCAR Inc is a global technology leader in the design, manufacture, and support of premium light-, medium-, and heavy-duty trucks under the Kenworth, Peterbilt, and DAF nameplates. The company also provides financial services, information technology, and distributes truck parts through its PACCAR Parts division. PACCAR is known for its premium quality products, strong balance sheet, and consistent profitability throughout economic cycles.
QCOM: Qualcomm Incorporated is a global leader in wireless technology and semiconductor design. The company develops and licenses wireless technology and designs and sells semiconductor products used in mobile devices, automotive, IoT, and computing. Qualcomm's Snapdragon processors power the majority of premium Android smartphones worldwide, and the company holds an extensive patent portfolio covering fundamental wireless communications technologies including 5G, 4G, and Wi-Fi.
DGX: Quest Diagnostics is the largest provider of diagnostic information services in the United States. The company offers a broad range of clinical laboratory testing services used by physicians, hospitals, managed care organizations, employers, and government agencies. Quest processes approximately 200 million test requisitions annually through its network of laboratories and patient service centers, making it a critical part of the U.S. healthcare infrastructure.
SSNC: SS&C Technologies Holdings is one of the largest global providers of financial technology software and services for the financial services and healthcare industries. The company offers cloud-based solutions for fund administration, portfolio management, accounting, risk and compliance, and healthcare data management. SS&C serves approximately 20,000 clients in the financial services industry, including hedge funds, asset managers, banks, and insurance companies.
STLD: Steel Dynamics is one of the largest domestic steel producers and metals recyclers in the United States. The company operates through three segments: Steel Operations, Metals Recycling, and Steel Fabrication. Steel Dynamics is recognized as an industry leader in operating efficiency and financial performance, utilizing electric arc furnace steelmaking technology. The company has consistently invested in growth and diversification while maintaining strong free cash flow generation.
SYK: Stryker Corporation is one of the world's leading medical technology companies. The company offers a diverse array of innovative medical products and services in orthopedics, medical and surgical, and neurotechnology and spine. Stryker's products include joint replacement implants, surgical equipment, neurovascular devices, and emergency medical equipment. The company has a strong track record of consistent growth through both organic innovation and strategic acquisitions.
TEL: TE Connectivity is a global industrial technology company and one of the world's largest manufacturers of connectors and sensors. The company designs and manufactures products that connect and protect the flow of power, data, and signal in a wide range of applications across transportation, industrial, and communications end markets. TE's products are essential components in automotive, aerospace, industrial equipment, and data communications infrastructure.
VLO: Valero Energy Corporation is the world's largest independent petroleum refiner and a major renewable fuels producer. The company operates 15 petroleum refineries with a combined throughput capacity of approximately 3.2 million barrels per day. Valero also operates 12 ethanol plants and a renewable diesel plant. The company is a leading producer of renewable diesel and sustainable aviation fuel, positioning it at the intersection of traditional energy and the energy transition.
AMD: Advanced Micro Devices is a global semiconductor company designing and selling high-performance computing, graphics, and visualization technologies. AMD's product portfolio includes Ryzen processors for PCs, EPYC processors for data centers, Radeon graphics cards, and Instinct accelerators for AI and machine learning workloads. The company's 2022 acquisition of Xilinx expanded its capabilities into adaptive computing. AMD has gained significant market share from Intel in both the PC and server markets.
AEM: Agnico Eagle Mines is one of the world's largest gold mining companies, with operations in Canada, Australia, Finland, and Mexico. The company has a strong track record of operational performance and financial discipline, consistently delivering robust gold production from its portfolio of high-quality, long-life mines. Agnico Eagle is known for its low-risk geographic profile, strong balance sheet, and commitment to responsible mining practices.
ATAT: Atour Lifestyle Holdings is a leading hotel group in China focused on the upper-midscale to midscale hotel segments. The company operates primarily under the Atour brand and manages a rapidly growing portfolio of hotels across major Chinese cities. Atour differentiates itself through its lifestyle-oriented approach, offering curated in-room amenities and retail products. The company has demonstrated strong unit growth and improving profitability as it scales its asset-light franchise model.
CVX: Chevron Corporation is one of the world's largest integrated energy companies, with operations spanning oil and gas exploration and production, refining, chemicals, and renewable energy. The company operates in virtually every segment of the energy industry and has significant assets in the Permian Basin, offshore Gulf of Mexico, and international locations including Australia and Kazakhstan. Chevron has increased its dividend for over 35 consecutive years and maintains one of the strongest balance sheets among the oil majors.
CWAN: Clearwater Analytics Holdings is a leading provider of SaaS-based investment accounting, reporting, and analytics solutions. The company's cloud-native platform automates the entire investment lifecycle for insurance companies, asset managers, corporations, and government entities. Clearwater aggregates data from over 1,800 custodians and data sources, providing a single source of truth for investment portfolios. The company has demonstrated strong recurring revenue growth since its founding in 2004.
CNX: CNX Resources Corporation is a leading Appalachian Basin natural gas producer focused on developing its substantial resource base in the Marcellus and Utica shale formations. The company is recognized for its low-cost production structure, strong free cash flow generation, and innovative approach to emissions reduction. CNX has been a pioneer in responsibly sourced natural gas and has committed to significant emissions reduction targets across its operations.
COIN: Coinbase Global is the largest cryptocurrency exchange in the United States and one of the largest globally by trading volume. The company provides a trusted platform for buying, selling, transferring, and storing digital assets for retail users, institutional investors, and developers. Coinbase generates revenue primarily from transaction fees, subscription and services revenue, and custodial services. The company went public via direct listing in April 2021.
EIX: Edison International is one of the largest electric utilities in the United States, serving approximately 5 million customers through its subsidiary Southern California Edison. The company provides electricity across a 50,000-square-mile service territory in central, coastal, and Southern California. Edison International is a leader in renewable energy procurement and grid modernization, with one of the cleanest generation portfolios among major U.S. utilities.
FINV: FinVolution Group is a leading fintech platform connecting underserved borrowers with financial institutions in China and internationally. The company uses proprietary AI and big data analytics to facilitate consumer lending, providing credit assessment and loan facilitation services. FinVolution has expanded beyond China into Indonesia, the Philippines, and other Southeast Asian markets, diversifying its revenue base and reducing country-specific risk.
FTNT: Fortinet is a global leader in cybersecurity, providing broad, integrated, and automated security solutions. The company's flagship FortiGate next-generation firewall platform is the most widely deployed network firewall in the world. Fortinet's Security Fabric architecture provides end-to-end protection across the entire digital attack surface, from network and cloud to endpoints and IoT. The company has consistently gained market share in the cybersecurity industry.
GFI: Gold Fields Limited is a globally diversified gold producer with operations in South Africa, Ghana, Australia, and Peru, and a project in Chile. The company is one of the world's largest gold mining companies by production volume. Gold Fields has been focused on extending mine life, improving operational efficiency, and developing its pipeline of growth projects. The company offers investors exposure to gold prices through a portfolio of high-quality, long-life mining assets.
SUPV: Grupo Supervielle is a leading financial services group in Argentina, providing banking, insurance, consumer finance, and asset management services. The company operates through Banco Supervielle, one of the largest private banks in Argentina, serving individuals, small and medium enterprises, and large corporations. Supervielle has been expanding its digital banking capabilities and fintech offerings to capture growth in Argentina's underbanked population.
HRMY: Harmony Biosciences Holdings is a commercial-stage pharmaceutical company focused on developing and commercializing therapies for patients with rare neurological diseases. The company's lead product, WAKIX (pitolisant), is the first and only FDA-approved treatment for excessive daytime sleepiness in adults with narcolepsy that works through a novel mechanism of action on histamine H3 receptors. Harmony has been expanding WAKIX's label and exploring additional indications.
HUT: Hut 8 Corp. is one of North America's largest Bitcoin mining companies, operating a fleet of mining facilities powered primarily by renewable and carbon-free energy sources. Following its merger with US Bitcoin Corp in 2023, the company expanded its operations significantly and diversified into high-performance computing and AI infrastructure services. Hut 8 holds one of the largest self-mined Bitcoin reserves of any publicly traded company.
KEYS: Keysight Technologies is a leading technology company that delivers advanced design, test, and measurement solutions. The company helps enterprises, service providers, and governments accelerate innovation in 5G, autonomous vehicles, IoT, and cybersecurity. Spun off from Agilent Technologies in 2014, Keysight has grown into the world's largest electronic test and measurement company, serving customers in communications, aerospace, defense, automotive, and semiconductor industries.
MARA: MARA Holdings (formerly Marathon Digital Holdings) is one of the largest Bitcoin mining companies in the world by hash rate. The company operates large-scale Bitcoin mining facilities across the United States and internationally, utilizing a mix of energy sources with an increasing focus on renewable energy. MARA has been actively expanding its mining capacity and diversifying its operations, including immersion cooling technology and energy harvesting initiatives.
NVDA: NVIDIA Corporation is the world's leading designer of graphics processing units (GPUs) and a dominant force in accelerated computing. Originally focused on gaming graphics, NVIDIA has become the most important company in the AI revolution, with its data center GPUs powering the vast majority of AI training and inference workloads globally. The company's CUDA software platform and ecosystem create a significant competitive moat. NVIDIA's market capitalization has made it one of the most valuable companies in the world.
NXPI: NXP Semiconductors is a global leader in secure connectivity solutions for embedded applications. The company provides semiconductor solutions for automotive, industrial, IoT, mobile, and communications infrastructure markets. NXP is the #1 semiconductor supplier to the automotive industry and a leader in secure identification, NFC, and RFID technology. The company's products are essential for advanced driver assistance systems, vehicle electrification, and the connected car ecosystem.
OKE: ONEOK, Inc. is one of the largest operators of natural gas gathering, processing, and transportation infrastructure in the United States. The company's extensive pipeline network connects key supply basins to major market centers across the Midcontinent and Rocky Mountain regions. ONEOK provides critical midstream services that support natural gas and NGL production. The company is known for its fee-based business model, strong distribution coverage, and consistent growth through infrastructure expansion.
PDD: PDD Holdings is a multinational commerce group that operates two major e-commerce platforms: Pinduoduo in China and Temu internationally. Pinduoduo revolutionized Chinese e-commerce with its social commerce and group-buying model, becoming one of the largest e-commerce platforms in China by users. Temu, launched in 2022, has rapidly expanded globally with an ultra-low-price direct-from-factory model, reaching hundreds of millions of users across North America, Europe, and other markets.
HOOD: Robinhood Markets is a financial services company that pioneered commission-free stock trading through its mobile app. The company offers trading in stocks, ETFs, options, and cryptocurrencies, as well as cash management and retirement accounts. Robinhood's mission is to democratize finance for all, and its easy-to-use platform has attracted a large base of younger, first-time investors. The company has expanded into gold subscriptions, credit cards, and wealth management services.
SYF: Synchrony Financial is one of the premier consumer financial services companies in the United States. The company provides a range of credit products through programs established with retailers, healthcare providers, auto dealers, and digital partners. Synchrony is the largest provider of private label credit cards in the U.S., partnering with major brands including Amazon, Lowe's, PayPal, and Walgreens. The company leverages data analytics and digital capabilities to drive engagement and growth.
TSM: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company is the world's largest dedicated semiconductor foundry, manufacturing chips designed by other companies. TSMC produces the most advanced chips in the world, including processors for Apple, NVIDIA, AMD, and Qualcomm. The company holds approximately 60% of the global foundry market share and dominates leading-edge chip production. TSMC's technology leadership in advanced process nodes (3nm, 5nm) makes it one of the most strategically important companies in the global technology supply chain.
TOL: Toll Brothers is the nation's leading builder of luxury homes, operating in 24 states across the United States. The company builds single-family detached and attached homes, master-planned communities, and urban high-rise condominiums. Toll Brothers targets the move-up and luxury segments of the housing market, with an average home selling price significantly above the national median. The company also operates mortgage, title, and insurance subsidiaries.
IBIT: iShares Bitcoin Trust is a spot Bitcoin exchange-traded fund managed by BlackRock, the world's largest asset manager. Launched in January 2024, IBIT provides investors with direct exposure to Bitcoin through a regulated, exchange-traded vehicle without the need to directly hold or custody the cryptocurrency. IBIT has become one of the fastest-growing ETFs in history, attracting tens of billions in assets within its first year of trading.
ETHA: iShares Ethereum Trust is a spot Ethereum exchange-traded fund managed by BlackRock. Launched in 2024, ETHA provides investors with direct exposure to Ethereum, the second-largest cryptocurrency by market capitalization, through a regulated exchange-traded vehicle. Ethereum is a decentralized blockchain platform that enables smart contracts and decentralized applications (dApps), serving as the foundation for much of the decentralized finance (DeFi) ecosystem.
IUSG: iShares Core S&P U.S. Growth ETF tracks the S&P 900 Growth Index, providing broad exposure to large- and mid-cap U.S. growth stocks. The fund emphasizes companies exhibiting above-average growth characteristics including sales growth, earnings growth, and price momentum. IUSG is one of the lowest-cost growth ETFs available, with a heavy weighting toward technology, consumer discretionary, and healthcare sectors.
DVY: iShares Select Dividend ETF tracks the Dow Jones U.S. Select Dividend Index, providing exposure to relatively high-dividend-paying U.S. equities. The fund selects stocks based on dividend yield, dividend growth rate, and payout ratio, screening for quality and sustainability. DVY offers diversified exposure across sectors including utilities, financials, energy, and consumer staples, making it a popular choice for income-focused investors.
IWS: iShares Russell Mid-Cap Value ETF tracks the Russell Midcap Value Index, providing exposure to mid-sized U.S. companies exhibiting value characteristics. The fund targets companies with lower price-to-book ratios and lower expected growth rates relative to the broader mid-cap market. IWS offers diversified exposure across sectors including financials, industrials, real estate, and healthcare, providing a complement to growth-oriented mid-cap strategies.
SPY: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust is the world's first and largest exchange-traded fund, tracking the S&P 500 Index. Launched in 1993, SPY provides broad exposure to 500 of the largest U.S. companies, representing approximately 80% of total U.S. equity market capitalization. It is the most actively traded security in the world by dollar volume and serves as the primary benchmark for U.S. large-cap equity performance.
QQQ: Invesco QQQ Trust tracks the Nasdaq-100 Index, providing exposure to 100 of the largest non-financial companies listed on the Nasdaq stock exchange. The fund is heavily weighted toward technology and growth-oriented companies including Apple, Microsoft, NVIDIA, Amazon, and Meta. QQQ is one of the most actively traded ETFs in the world and is widely used as a benchmark for large-cap growth and technology sector performance.
DIA: SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF Trust tracks the Dow Jones Industrial Average, one of the oldest and most widely recognized stock market indices in the world. The fund provides exposure to 30 large, well-established U.S. companies across various industries, selected by the editors of The Wall Street Journal. DIA is a price-weighted index, meaning higher-priced stocks have greater influence on the index's performance.
NTR: Nutrien Ltd. is the world's largest provider of crop inputs and services, producing and distributing nitrogen, potash, and phosphate fertilizers. Headquartered in Saskatoon, Saskatchewan, the company operates extensive mining and manufacturing facilities across North America and owns the largest agricultural retail network globally, with approximately 2,000 farm supply locations. Nutrien plays a critical role in global food security, as its products underpin roughly half of the world's crop yields.
CTRA: Coterra Energy Inc. is an independent oil and natural gas exploration and production company operating in two of America's premier basins: the Permian Basin in West Texas (oil-focused) and the Marcellus Shale in Appalachia (natural gas-focused). This dual-basin strategy provides diversified exposure across both oil and gas commodity cycles. The company is known for disciplined capital allocation, low-cost operations, and a shareholder-friendly return framework that combines a base dividend with variable distributions.
FCX: Freeport-McMoRan Inc. is the world's largest publicly traded copper mining company, operating the massive Grasberg mine in Indonesia — one of the largest copper and gold deposits on earth — alongside significant operations in Arizona and Peru. Copper is essential to electrification, renewable energy, electric vehicles, and data center power infrastructure. With new copper mines requiring 10-15 years to develop, Freeport's existing production capacity positions it as a critical supplier in a structurally supply-constrained market.
CRDO: Credo Technology Group designs and sells high-speed connectivity semiconductors for data centers. The company's SerDes (serializer/deserializer) chips and active electrical cables enable data to move between GPUs and switches at extreme speeds inside AI training clusters. As AI data centers scale to hundreds of thousands of processors, the interconnect fabric between them — not the processors themselves — becomes the performance bottleneck. Credo's products sit at exactly that chokepoint, driving revenue from $107 million to over $1.3 billion in annual run rate in approximately three years.
VST: Vistra Corp. is one of the largest independent power producers in the United States, operating a diverse fleet of natural gas, nuclear, coal, and renewable generation assets. The company owns the second-largest competitive nuclear fleet in the country, providing 24/7 zero-emission baseload power. With U.S. electricity demand expected to grow 50% by 2040 driven by AI data centers, manufacturing reshoring, and electric vehicles, Vistra has secured 20-year power purchase agreements with major technology companies including Meta and Amazon for over 2,600 megawatts of nuclear capacity.
MRVL: Marvell Technology Inc. is a leading semiconductor company focused on data infrastructure. Approximately three-quarters of its revenue comes from data center customers, where Marvell designs custom AI accelerator chips (XPUs) for hyperscale cloud companies including Alphabet, Amazon, and Microsoft. The company also produces industry-standard PAM4 DSP chips that convert data between electrical and optical signals — critical technology as data centers transition from copper to fiber optic interconnects. Marvell's acquisition of Celestial AI adds photonic fabric technology that uses light to move data between chips on the same board.
